conversation.transcript:
Student: Hey, have you heard about the new library hours?
Librarian: Yes, they extended them on weekends!
Student: That’s great! What time do they close now?
Librarian: They’re open until 10 PM on Saturdays and Sundays.

questions.q: What change was made to the library hours?
questions.explain: 圖書館延長了周末的開放時間。

questions.q: When do the library hours end on weekends?
questions.explain: 圖書館在周末的關閉時間是晚上10點。

announcement.transcript: Attention students: The annual campus festival will take place this Friday from 4 PM to 11 PM on the main quad. There will be food trucks, live music, and games. Don’t forget to bring your student ID for discounts!

questions.q: What event is happening this Friday?
questions.explain: 本週五將舉行校園嘉年華。

questions.q: What should students bring to the event?
questions.explain: 學生應該攜帶學生證以獲得折扣。

talk.transcript: Today, I will explain the concept of sustainable development. It refers to meeting the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs. This involves balancing economic growth, environmental care, and social well-being. Sustainable development encourages us to use resources efficiently, reduce waste, and promote renewable energy sources. It's a holistic approach that aims to create a better quality of life for all while protecting our planet for future generations.

questions.q: What is the main goal of sustainable development?
options: To meet present and future needs.
questions.explain: 可持續發展的主要目標是滿足當前和未來的需求。

options: Balancing economic growth and care for the environment.
questions.explain: 可持續發展鼓勵平衡經濟增長和對環境的關注。

questions.q: What type of energy sources does sustainable development promote?
questions.explain: 可持續發展提倡使用可再生能源。

questions.q: Why is sustainable development considered holistic?
options: It focuses on economic growth only.
It integrates various aspects of society.
It is only about reducing costs.
questions.explain: 可持續發展被認為是全面的，因為它整合了社會的各個方面。
